questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Which of the following is incorrect relationship between given concentration terms?
$\mathbf{M}=$ molarity; $\mathbf{m}=$ molality; $\mathbf{d}=$ density of solution in $\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{mL}$
$M^{\prime}=$ molar mass of solute $\left(\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}\right) ; \mathbf{M}^{\prime \prime}=$ molar mass of solvent $\left(\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}\right)$
$X^{\prime}=$ mole fraction of solute; $\mathrm{X}^{\prime \prime}=$ mole fraction of solvent
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & m=\frac{M \times 1000}{\left(1000 d-M^{\prime} \times M\right)} \\ & \frac{1}{m}=\frac{d}{M}-\frac{M^{\prime}}{1000}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & M=\frac{n_{\text {solute }} \times d \times 1000}{w t_{\text {solution }}}=\frac{n_{\text {solute }} \times d \times 1000}{n_{\text {solvent }} M^{\prime \prime}+n_{\text {solute }} M^{\prime}} \\ & M=\frac{x_{\text {solute }} \times d \times 1000}{x_{\text {solvent }} M^{\prime \prime}+x_{\text {solute }} M^{\prime}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: List I contain different solution in which solute is non-volatile while List II contain their related property (F.P and B.P of water are 273 K and 373 K while $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{f}}$ and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}$ of water are $1.86 \mathrm{Km}^{-1}$ and $0.53 \mathrm{Km}^{-1}$ respectively)
questions.solution:
If mole fraction of solute $=0.1$ then molality $=\frac{0.1 \times 1000}{0.9 \times 18}=6.17$

If solute is electrolyte ( $A B_2$ ) with $\alpha=0.5$ then $\mathrm{i}=1+2 \alpha \Rightarrow 2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: What will be the correct order of vapour pressure of water, acetone and ether at $30^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Given that among these compounds, water has maximum boiling point and ether has minimum boiling point?
questions.solution: Greater the boiling point, less is the vapour pressure. Hence, the correct order of vapour pressure wil be Water  < acetone < ether
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The values of observed and calculated molecular weights of silver nitrate are 92.64 and 170 respectively. The degree of dissociation of silver nitrate is
questions.solution:
i for $\mathrm{AgNO}_3=\frac{\text { normal molecular weight }}{\text { observed molecular weight }}=1+\alpha$
$\alpha=\frac{170}{92.64}-1=0.835=83.5 \%$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
List I contain pair of solute and solvent and List II contain properties of their resulting solution.
\(\begin{array}{llll} \text{List-I} & \text{List-II} \\ \text{(P) n-hexane +n-Heptane} & \text{(1) } \Delta H_{\text {mixing }}=0 \\ \text{(Q) } \mathrm{CS}_2+ \text{Acetone} & \text{(2) } \Delta H_{\text {mixing }} \neq 0 \\ \text{(R) } \mathrm{CHCl}_3+ \text{Acetone} & \text{(3) Minimum boiling azeotrope can be formed} \\ \text{(S) Benzene + toluene} & \text{(4) Maximum boiling azeotrope can be formed} \\ & \text{(5) } P_S=P_A^{\circ} X_A+P_B^{\circ} X_B \\ & \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{S}} \rightarrow \text{ vapour pressure of solution} \\ & \mathrm{P}_A^{\circ} \rightarrow \text{ vapour pressure of pure solute} \\ & \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{B}}^{\circ} \rightarrow \text{ vapour pressure of pure solvent} \\ & X_A \rightarrow \text{ mole fraction of solute} \\ & X_B \rightarrow \text{ mole fraction of solvent} \end{array}\)
questions.solution:
n -Hexane +n -Heptane $\rightarrow$ Ideal solution
Benzene + Toluene $\rightarrow$ Ideal solution
$\mathrm{CS}_2+$ Acetone $\rightarrow$ Non-ideal with positive deviation
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
